title: cynoglossum pustulatum subsp. soilae
intro: Cynoglossum pustulatum subsp. soilae, also known as the alpine gromwell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The alpine gromwell has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant effects, making it a valuable treatment for respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe respiratory tract irritations, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and asthma. Alpine gromwell can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in traditional medicine to support respiratory health and relieve coughs.
